title: Websites like Purdue OWL and Grammarly are great options.
content:
Websites like Purdue OWL and Grammarly are great options.
Check for one type of error at a time—don’t overwhelm yourself.
You are smarter than your word processor—don’t rely on it to correct mistakes.
Check your formatting, particularly APA formatting.
Ensure you include an APA title page with all the required items.
Make sure you have used an APA in-text citation every time you use source information in your essay, whether it be a direct quote or paraphrase.
Check your APA References page to confirm that all your sources are cited correctly.
An extra set of eyes always helps. Swap papers with a friend or a classmate, or pick a couple of error types to focus on. Use the Brainfuse services available!
A comprehensive editing checklistLinks to an external site. could be helpful for both essays.
After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Editing.

Week 6 | Editing

Editing Your Work
Editing goes beyond proofreading. It involves a more comprehensive evaluation of the piece's content, organization, and structure. It aims to refine the message, coherence, and logical flow of ideas. During the editing phase, writers assess the clarity of their arguments, the strength of their supporting evidence, and the effectiveness of their transitions between paragraphs and sections. They may rephrase sentences to improve clarity, reorganize paragraphs to enhance ideas' logical progression, and remove or add information to ensure the piece is well-balanced and comprehensive. Editing requires a critical eye, attention to detail, and the willingness to make substantial revisions to enhance the overall quality of the writing.
